title: Wait-One-Lap Safety-Car Option Value
summary: Both F1 coverage and the dynamic-programming literature note that yellow flags and Safety Cars materially cheapen pit stops; current features capture tyre stress but not the option value of waiting because a neutralization may arrive soon.
rationale: Both F1 coverage and the dynamic-programming literature note that yellow flags and Safety Cars materially cheapen pit stops; current features capture tyre stress but not the option value of waiting because a neutralization may arrive soon.
implementation_hint: Estimate train-only circuit-lap neutralization priors or pit-burst priors, then derive `expected_pit_loss_if_wait_1` versus `pit_now` features. Interact those with track position, laps remaining, and current tyre distress to model when teams rationally delay.
expected_effect: Should reduce false positives on rows where a stop is due soon but the strategically correct action is to hold out for a cheaper window.
risk: Historical neutralization priors can be unstable across seasons and must be built without peeking across the full race.
